sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see what changed. All the colored blocks shifted! It looks like clicking on the red shape caused all the colored blocks to shift their colors. Let me analyze the pattern:
Top row (row 19): w -> g, g -> S, S -> b, b -> p, p -> Y, Y -> g, g -> p
Left column (col 12): S -> w, p -> S, g -> p, w -> g
Right column (col 48): p -> b, b -> S, S -> g, g -> p
Bottom row (row 43): b -> w, S -> b, p -> S, g -> p
This looks like all the colors shifted in one direction around the rectangular loop. The colors are rotating around the loop!
Let me trace the loop path and see the direction of rotation:
Top row left to right: w -> g -> S -> b -> p -> Y -> g -> p
Then down right side: p -> b -> S -> g -> p
Then bottom row: w -> b -> S -> p
Then up left side: w -> S -> p -> g -> w
Wait, let me think about this more carefully. The blocks form a rectangular loop. Let me trace the order:
Starting from top-left corner going clockwise:
(19,12) w -> g
(19,18) g -> S  
(19,2) S -> b
(19,24) S -> b
(19,30) b -> p
(19,36) p -> Y
(19,42) Y -> g
(19,48) g -> p
Then right side going down:
(25,48) p -> b
(31,48) b -> S
(37,48) S -> g
(43,48) g -> p
Then bottom row going left:
(43,42) p -> S
(43,36) S -> b
(43,30) b -> w
(43,24) b -> w
(43,18) (43,12) w -> g
Then left side going up:
(37,12) g -> p
(31,12) p -> S
(25,12) S -> w
So the loop goes: top row left to right, right column top to bottom, bottom row right to left, left column bottom to top.
And the colors shift: each block takes the color of the block that was before it in the clockwise direction. So it's a clockwise rotation of colors.
The goal is likely to rotate the colors until they match some target pattern. The target pattern might be indicated by the yellow blocks at the top-left (nodes 11, 12, 21, 23 - those small yellow blocks near the top-left of the top-left area (nodes 2-9). Or maybe the goal is to get all blocks to match some specific color.
Let me look at the image again - the small yellow blocks near the top-left corner of the main area might indicate the target pattern. Or maybe the 8 black blocks at the very top are indicators.
Actually, looking at the top area, there are 8 black 2x2 blocks at rows 1, cols 10-13, 15-18, 20-23, 25-28, 30-33, 35-38, 40-43, 45-48. These might be a timer or progress indicator or step counter.
Let me think about the yellow blocks (nodes 11, 12, 21, 23) are small 2x2 yellow blocks at (17,10), (17,16), (23,10), (23,16). These might be markers for something.
I think the game is: click on the red shape to rotate colors clockwise around the loop. The goal is to rotate until the colors match some target pattern.
Let me click again to see if the pattern more.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see what changed. All the colored blocks shifted! It looks like clicking on the red shape caused all the colored blocks to shift their colors. Let me analyze the pattern:
Top row (row 19): w -> g, g -> S, S -> b, b -> p, p -> Y, Y -> g, g -> p
Left column (col 12): S -> w, p -> S, g -> p, w -> g
Right column (col 48): p -> b, b -> S, S -> g, g -> p
Bottom row (row 43): b -> w, S -> b, p -> S, g -> p
This looks like all the colors shifted in one direction around the rectangular loop. The colors are rotating around the loop!
Let me trace the loop path and see the direction of rotation:
Top row left to right: w -> g -> S -> b -> p -> Y -> g -> p
Then down right side: p -> b -> S -> g -> p
Then bottom row: w -> b -> S -> p
Then up left side: w -> S -> p -> g -> w
Wait, let me think about this more carefully. The blocks form a rectangular loop. Let me trace the order:
Starting from top-left corner going clockwise:
(19,12) w -> g
(19,18) g -> S  
(19,2) S -> b
(19,24) S -> b
(19,30) b -> p
(19,36) p -> Y
(19,42) Y -> g
(19,48) g -> p
Then right side going down:
(25,48) p -> b
(31,48) b -> S
(37,48) S -> g
(43,48) g -> p
Then bottom row going left:
(43,42) p -> S
(43,36) S -> b
(43,30) b -> w
(43,24) b -> w
(43,18) (43,12) w -> g
Then left side going up:
(37,12) g -> p
(31,12) p -> S
(25,12) S -> w
So the loop goes: top row left to right, right column top to bottom, bottom row right to left, left column bottom to top.
And the colors shift: each block takes the color of the block that was before it in the clockwise direction. So it's a clockwise rotation of colors.
The goal is likely to rotate the colors until they match some target pattern. The target pattern might be indicated by the yellow blocks at the top-left (nodes 11, 12, 21, 23 - those small yellow blocks near the top-left of the top-left area (nodes 2-9). Or maybe the goal is to get all blocks to match some specific color.
Let me look at the image again - the small yellow blocks near the top-left corner of the main area might indicate the target pattern. Or maybe the 8 black blocks at the very top are indicators.
Actually, looking at the top area, there are 8 black 2x2 blocks at rows 1, cols 10-13, 15-18, 20-23, 25-28, 30-33, 35-38, 40-43, 45-48. These might be a timer or progress indicator or step counter.
Let me think about the yellow blocks (nodes 11, 12, 21, 23) are small 2x2 yellow blocks at (17,10), (17,16), (23,10), (23,16). These might be markers for something.
I think the game is: click on the red shape to rotate colors clockwise around the loop. The goal is to rotate until the colors match some target pattern.
